{"thought": "SITUATION: Scan complete on 192.168.56.101. This is a classic Metasploitable2 VM with multiple CRITICAL vulnerabilities. Key findings: vsftpd 2.3.4 (backdoor CVE-2011-2523), UnrealIRCd (backdoor CVE-2010-2075), Samba 3.0.20 (usermap_script CVE-2007-2447), distccd (CVE-2004-2687), plus MySQL, PostgreSQL, VNC, R-services. HYPOTHESIS: All four CRITICAL CVEs have reliable public exploits and should yield shells. OPTIONS: A) Spawn all exploit agents in parallel - maximum coverage, first shell wins. B) Try one by one - wastes time, unnecessary serialization. DECISION: Option A. Spawning exploit agents for vsftpd, unrealircd, samba, and distccd simultaneously. Per my playbook, these exact exploits have been successful before. Webapp agent also spawned to scan port 80/8180 in parallel since web enumeration is slow.", "action": "spawn_agents_batch", "parameters": {"agents": [{"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_vsftpd", "options": {"port": 21}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_unrealircd", "options": {"port": 6667}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_samba", "options": {"port": 445}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_distccd", "options": {"port": 3632}}, {"agent_type": "webapp", "target": "http://192.168.56.101", "task_type": "web_scan_full", "options": {}}, {"agent_type": "webapp", "target": "http://192.168.56.101:8180", "task_type": "web_scan_full", "options": {}}]}}
